name: Audit Asana task hygiene and log a weekly scorecard to Google Sheets and Slack

parameters.content:
## Audit Asana task hygiene and log a weekly scorecard to Google Sheets and Slack

Scans one Asana project once a week for open tasks that are missing an assignee or a due date (with optional section and description checks), logs every flagged task to a Google Sheet with comma-joined reason codes, and posts a field-completeness scorecard to Slack. It reads from Asana over the REST API and writes nothing back into Asana.

### How it works

1. A schedule trigger fires every Monday morning and a Set node holds the project, sheet link, Slack channel, and two optional check toggles.
2. The project's tasks are fetched read-only from the Asana API, then split into one item per task and filtered down to open tasks only.
3. A Code node scores each open task and emits one row per flagged task with its reason codes joined by commas.
4. Every flagged task is appended to the audit sheet, one row per task.
5. A second Code node computes the completeness numbers and posts a scorecard to Slack with the top offenders and a link to the sheet.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Add an Asana Personal Access Token credential and select it on `Fetch Asana Tasks`.
- [ ] Open `Set Audit Config` and set `projectGid`, `slackChannel`, and `auditSheetUrl`.
- [ ] Connect a Google Sheets credential and pick the spreadsheet and tab on `Append Audit Rows in Sheets`.
- [ ] Connect a Slack credential on `Post Scorecard to Slack`.
- [ ] Add the header row to your audit sheet: audit_date, task_gid, task_name, assignee, section, due_on, reason_codes, permalink_url.

### Customization

Turn on `checkSection` or `checkDescription` in `Set Audit Config` to also flag tasks with no section or an empty description. Change the Monday 8am schedule to any cadence.

## Trigger and configure

The schedule fires weekly. `Set Audit Config` is the one place you set the project, the Slack channel, the audit sheet link, and the two optional check toggles.

## Read Asana read-only

Fetches the project's tasks over the Asana REST API with the fields the audit needs (assignee, due date, section, notes). This is a read. Nothing is ever written back to Asana.

## Score hygiene and log flagged tasks

Split the task list, keep only open tasks, then score each one. A task that fails any enabled check becomes one audit row with its reason codes joined by commas, appended to the sheet.

## Build and post the scorecard

Every number is computed in code from the open tasks: assignee coverage, due-date coverage, percent fully fielded, and the top offenders. The scorecard posts to Slack every run, even a clean week.

## Optional: plain-English summary

The scorecard is fully deterministic. If you want a one-line human summary on top of the numbers (for example "assignee coverage slipped this week"), add a Groq HTTP node after `Build Slack Scorecard` that reads the computed counts and prepends its sentence to the Slack text. Keep the model out of the scoring path: every hard number stays computed in the Code nodes. Off by default.
